title: Source to Shore beta vertical slice
summary: Implement the first Source to Shore proof as a narrow post-High-Pass Route v2 outing that connects high rime/source clues to first shelter at Treeline Pass, then files through the existing station flow.

execution_order.goal: Narrow Source to Shore to a first vertical slice that reuses existing Route v2 and station seams.
execution_order.why_now: The beta should prove one relationship loop before opening all five biomes.

execution_order.goal: Implement the Source Shelter route, station/map/journal handoff, and debug snapshots.
execution_order.why_now: The user asked to proceed directly to the Source to Shore beta vertical slice after the gate passed.

execution_order.goal: Review the slice for scope, readability, route-state stability, and future handoff clarity.
execution_order.why_now: The first beta slice should remain small, test-backed, and aligned with the five-biome relationship thesis.

completion_gate: Filed High Pass opens Source Shelter instead of a dead-end filed state.
Source Shelter can become active, ready-to-file, and filed through existing Route v2 progress.
Station, map, journal, atlas, and route marker surfaces agree on the Source Shelter target.
Debug snapshots exist for active, ready-to-file, and filed Source to Shore states.
guardrails: No new planner, dashboard, inventory, economy, combat, or direct API mode.
Keep copy compact for the 256x160 handheld surface.
Defer wider five-biome Source to Shore expansion until this vertical slice is verified.
post_packet_path: Run full validation, tests, build, and alpha RC after implementation.
Next Source to Shore work should deepen forest hold/release only after this slice remains green.
